lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਵੇਖਣੁ ਬੋਲਣਾ ਨਾਮੁ ਜਪਤ ਸੁਖੁ ਪਾਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: guramukh vekhan bolanaa naam japat sukh paaeaa |
lines.english: The Gurmukh beholds and speaks the Naam, the Name of the Lord; chanting the Naam, he finds peace.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਗਿਆਨੁ ਪ੍ਰਗਾਸਿਆ ਤਿਮਰ ਅਗਿਆਨੁ ਅੰਧੇਰੁ ਚੁਕਾਇਆ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: naanak guramukh giaan pragaasiaa timar agiaan andher chukaaeaa |2|
lines.english: O Nanak, the spiritual wisdom of the Gurmukh shines forth; the black darkness of ignorance is dispelled. ||2||

lines.translit: mahalaa 3 |
lines.english: Third Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਨਮੁਖ ਮੈਲੇ ਮਰਹਿ ਗਵਾਰ ॥
lines.translit: manamukh maile mareh gavaar |
lines.english: The filthy, foolish, self-willed manmukhs die.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਤਿਗੁਰੁ ਸੇਵਿਹੁ ਹਉਮੈ ਮਲੁ ਜਾਈ ॥
lines.translit: satigur sevihu haumai mal jaaee |
lines.english: Serve the True Guru, and the filth of your ego shall be gone.

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਨਿਰਮਲ ਹਰਿ ਰਾਖਿਆ ਉਰ ਧਾਰਿ ॥
lines.translit: guramukh niramal har raakhiaa ur dhaar |
lines.english: The Gurmukhs are immaculate and pure; they keep the Lord enshrined within their hearts.

lines.gurmukhi: ਭਨਤਿ ਨਾਨਕੁ ਸੁਣਹੁ ਜਨ ਭਾਈ ॥
lines.translit: bhanat naanak sunahu jan bhaaee |
lines.english: Prays Nanak, listen, O Siblings of Destiny!

lines.gurmukhi: ਦੂਜੈ ਭਾਇ ਸੂਤੇ ਕਬਹੁ ਨ ਜਾਗਹਿ ਮਾਇਆ ਮੋਹ ਪਿਆਰ ॥
lines.translit: doojai bhaae soote kabahu na jaageh maaeaa moh piaar |
lines.english: Asleep in the love of duality, they never wake up; they are attached to the love of Maya.

lines.gurmukhi: ਅੰਦਰਿ ਸੰਸਾ ਦੂਖੁ ਵਿਆਪੇ ਸਿਰਿ ਧੰਧਾ ਨਿਤ ਮਾਰ ॥
lines.translit: andar sansaa dookh viaape sir dhandhaa nit maar |
lines.english: Deep within, the pain of skepticism afflicts them; their heads are constantly assaulted by worldly entanglements.

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਰਿ ਨਾਮੁ ਨ ਭਾਇਆ ਬਿਰਥਾ ਜਨਮੁ ਗਵਾਇਆ ਨਾਨਕ ਜਮੁ ਮਾਰਿ ਕਰੇ ਖੁਆਰ ॥੩॥
lines.translit: har naam na bhaaeaa birathaa janam gavaaeaa naanak jam maar kare khuaar |3|
lines.english: They do not love the Lord's Name, and they lose their life uselessly. O Nanak, the Messenger of Death attacks them, and humiliates them. ||3||

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਮੁ ਨ ਚੇਤਹਿ ਸਬਦੁ ਨ ਵੀਚਾਰਹਿ ਇਹੁ ਮਨਮੁਖ ਕਾ ਬੀਚਾਰ ॥
lines.translit: naam na cheteh sabad na veechaareh ihu manamukh kaa beechaar |
lines.english: They do not remember the Name, and they do not contemplate the Word of the Shabad; this is the view of the self-willed manmukhs.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਸ ਨੋ ਹਰਿ ਭਗਤਿ ਸਚੁ ਬਖਸੀਅਨੁ ਸੋ ਸਚਾ ਸਾਹੁ ॥
lines.translit: jis no har bhagat sach bakhaseean so sachaa saahu |
lines.english: He alone is a true king, whom the Lord blesses with true devotion.

lines.gurmukhi: ਪਉੜੀ ॥
lines.translit: paurree |
lines.english: Pauree:

lines.gurmukhi: ਤਿਸ ਕੀ ਮੁਹਤਾਜੀ ਲੋਕੁ ਕਢਦਾ ਹੋਰਤੁ ਹਟਿ ਨ ਵਥੁ ਨ ਵੇਸਾਹੁ ॥
lines.translit: tis kee muhataajee lok kadtadaa horat hatt na vath na vesaahu |
lines.english: People pledge their allegiance to him; no other store stocks this merchandise, nor deals in this trade.

lines.gurmukhi: ਭਗਤ ਜਨਾ ਕਉ ਸਨਮੁਖੁ ਹੋਵੈ ਸੁ ਹਰਿ ਰਾਸਿ ਲਏ ਵੇਮੁਖ ਭਸੁ ਪਾਹੁ ॥
lines.translit: bhagat janaa kau sanamukh hovai su har raas le vemukh bhas paahu |
lines.english: That humble devotee who turns his face towards the Guru and becomes sunmukh, receives the Lord's wealth; the faithless baymukh, who turns his face away from the Guru, gathers only ashes.

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਰਿ ਕੇ ਨਾਮ ਕੇ ਵਾਪਾਰੀ ਹਰਿ ਭਗਤ ਹਹਿ ਜਮੁ ਜਾਗਾਤੀ ਤਿਨਾ ਨੇੜਿ ਨ ਜਾਹੁ ॥
lines.translit: har ke naam ke vaapaaree har bhagat heh jam jaagaatee tinaa nerr na jaahu |
lines.english: The Lord's devotees are dealers in the Name of the Lord. The Messenger of Death, the tax-collector, does not even approach them.

lines.gurmukhi: ਇਸੁ ਜੁਗ ਮਹਿ ਭਗਤੀ ਹਰਿ ਧਨੁ ਖਟਿਆ ਹੋਰੁ ਸਭੁ ਜਗਤੁ ਭਰਮਿ ਭੁਲਾਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: eis jug meh bhagatee har dhan khattiaa hor sabh jagat bharam bhulaaeaa |
lines.english: In this age, the devotee earns the wealth of the Lord; all the rest of the world wanders deluded in doubt.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਲੋਕ ਮਃ ੩ ॥
lines.translit: salok mahalaa 3 |
lines.english: Salok, Third Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਨ ਨਾਨਕਿ ਹਰਿ ਨਾਮ ਧਨੁ ਲਦਿਆ ਸਦਾ ਵੇਪਰਵਾਹੁ ॥੭॥
lines.translit: jan naanak har naam dhan ladiaa sadaa veparavaahu |7|
lines.english: Servant Nanak has loaded the wealth of the Name of the Lord, who is forever independent and care-free. ||7||

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰ ਪਰਸਾਦੀ ਨਾਮੁ ਮਨਿ ਵਸਿਆ ਅਨਦਿਨੁ ਨਾਮੁ ਧਿਆਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: gur parasaadee naam man vasiaa anadin naam dhiaaeaa |
lines.english: By Guru's Grace, the Naam, the Name of the Lord, comes to dwell in his mind; night and day, he meditates on the Naam.

lines.gurmukhi: ਬਿਖਿਆ ਮਾਹਿ ਉਦਾਸ ਹੈ ਹਉਮੈ ਸਬਦਿ ਜਲਾਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: bikhiaa maeh udaas hai haumai sabad jalaaeaa |
lines.english: In the midst of corruption, he remains detached; through the Word of the Shabad, he burns away his ego.

lines.gurmukhi: ਆਪਿ ਤਰਿਆ ਕੁਲ ਉਧਰੇ ਧੰਨੁ ਜਣੇਦੀ ਮਾਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: aap tariaa kul udhare dhan janedee maaeaa |
lines.english: He crosses over, and saves his relatives as well; blessed is the mother who gave birth to him.

lines.gurmukhi: ਬ੍ਰਹਮਾ ਬਿਸਨੁ ਮਹਾਦੇਉ ਤ੍ਰੈ ਗੁਣ ਭੁਲੇ ਹਉਮੈ ਮੋਹੁ ਵਧਾਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: brahamaa bisan mahaadeo trai gun bhule haumai mohu vadhaaeaa |
lines.english: Brahma, Vishnu and Shiva wander in the three qualities, while their egotism and desire increase.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਦਾ ਸਹਜੁ ਸੁਖੁ ਮਨਿ ਵਸਿਆ ਸਚੇ ਸਿਉ ਲਿਵ ਲਾਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: sadaa sehaj sukh man vasiaa sache siau liv laaeaa |
lines.english: Peace and poise fill his mind forever, and he embraces love for the True Lord.

lines.gurmukhi: ਪੰਡਿਤ ਪੜਿ ਪੜਿ ਮੋਨੀ ਭੁਲੇ ਦੂਜੈ ਭਾਇ ਚਿਤੁ ਲਾਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: panddit parr parr monee bhule doojai bhaae chit laaeaa |
lines.english: The Pandits, the religious scholars and the silent sages read and debate in confusion; their consciousness is centered on the love of duality.

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਨਮੁਖ ਦੁਖੀਏ ਸਦਾ ਭ੍ਰਮਿ ਭੁਲੇ ਤਿਨੑੀ ਬਿਰਥਾ ਜਨਮੁ ਗਵਾਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: manamukh dukhee sadaa bhram bhule tinaee birathaa janam gavaaeaa |
lines.english: The miserable self-willed manmukhs are forever deluded by doubt; they waste away their lives uselessly.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੋਗੀ ਜੰਗਮ ਸੰਨਿਆਸੀ ਭੁਲੇ ਵਿਣੁ ਗੁਰ ਤਤੁ ਨ ਪਾਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: jogee jangam saniaasee bhule vin gur tat na paaeaa |
lines.english: The Yogis, wandering pilgrims and Sanyaasees are deluded; without the Guru, they do not find the essence of reality.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਸੋ ਸਾਲਾਹੀਐ ਜਿਸੁ ਵਸਿ ਸਭੁ ਕਿਛੁ ਹੋਇ ॥
lines.translit: naanak so saalaaheeai jis vas sabh kichh hoe |
lines.english: O Nanak, praise Him, who has control over everything.

lines.translit: mahalaa 3 |
lines.english: Third Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਨਾਮਿ ਰਤੇ ਸੇਈ ਜਨ ਸਮਧੇ ਜਿ ਆਪੇ ਬਖਸਿ ਮਿਲਾਇਆ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: naanak naam rate seee jan samadhe ji aape bakhas milaaeaa |1|
lines.english: O Nanak, those who are imbued with the Naam are balanced and poised; forgiving them, the Lord blends them with Himself. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਤਿਸਹਿ ਸਰੇਵਹੁ ਪ੍ਰਾਣੀਹੋ ਤਿਸੁ ਬਿਨੁ ਅਵਰੁ ਨ ਕੋਇ ॥
lines.translit: tiseh sarevahu praaneeho tis bin avar na koe |
lines.english: Remember Him, O mortals - without Him, there is no other at all.

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਅੰਤਰਿ ਮਨਿ ਵਸੈ ਸਦਾ ਸਦਾ ਸੁਖੁ ਹੋਇ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: guramukh antar man vasai sadaa sadaa sukh hoe |2|
lines.english: He dwells deep within those who are Gurmukh; forever and ever, they are at peace. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਨੀ ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਹਰਿ ਨਾਮ ਧਨੁ ਨ ਖਟਿਓ ਸੇ ਦੇਵਾਲੀਏ ਜੁਗ ਮਾਹਿ ॥
lines.translit: jinee guramukh har naam dhan na khattio se devaalee jug maeh |
lines.english: Those who do not become Gurmukh and earn the wealth of the Lord's Name, are bankrupt in this age.

lines.gurmukhi: ਪਉੜੀ ॥
lines.translit: paurree |
lines.english: Pauree:

lines.gurmukhi: ਪਰਾਈ ਬਖੀਲੀ ਕਰਹਿ ਆਪਣੀ ਪਰਤੀਤਿ ਖੋਵਨਿ ਸਗਵਾ ਭੀ ਆਪੁ ਲਖਾਹਿ ॥
lines.translit: paraaee bakheelee kareh aapanee parateet khovan sagavaa bhee aap lakhaeh |
lines.english: They gossip about others, and lose their credit, and expose themselves as well.

lines.gurmukhi: ਓਇ ਮੰਗਦੇ ਫਿਰਹਿ ਸਭ ਜਗਤ ਮਹਿ ਕੋਈ ਮੁਹਿ ਥੁਕ ਨ ਤਿਨ ਕਉ ਪਾਹਿ ॥
lines.translit: oe mangade fireh sabh jagat meh koee muhi thuk na tin kau paeh |
lines.english: They wander around begging all over the world, but no one even spits in their faces.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਸੁ ਧਨ ਕਾਰਣਿ ਚੁਗਲੀ ਕਰਹਿ ਸੋ ਧਨੁ ਚੁਗਲੀ ਹਥਿ ਨ ਆਵੈ ਓਇ ਭਾਵੈ ਤਿਥੈ ਜਾਹਿ ॥
lines.translit: jis dhan kaaran chugalee kareh so dhan chugalee hath na aavai oe bhaavai tithai jaeh |
lines.english: That wealth, for which they slander others, does not come into their hands, no matter where they go.
